title: Listening - Part 3: A Live Performance
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about a live performance they attended. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H what made the performance memorable for each speaker. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about a live performance they attended. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H what made the performance memorable for each speaker. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about seeing a live performance.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: I went to hear a string quartet while on holiday. Classical concerts are not unusual for me because my parents often take me, and the musicians were excellent, as expected. [19]What made this evening different was that they performed inside a huge cave, where small lights illuminated the rock walls and every note echoed around the natural space.[/19] It felt completely separate from ordinary life. Even people who didn't usually listen to classical music seemed unable to take their eyes off the performers.||Speaker 2 Man: During a school production, the microphone used by the main actor stopped working in an important scene. At first everyone in the audience looked uncomfortable, because we assumed the show would have to pause. [20]Instead, the other actors immediately moved closer, adjusted their own positions and continued so naturally that the interruption became part of the drama rather than ruining it.[/20] I admired their calmness more than any individual line or song. It showed how professional young performers can be.||Speaker 3 Woman: I attended a comedy show in a small café rather than a large theatre. I’d seen comedians on television, but live comedy usually seemed expensive and distant. Our seats were directly beside the tiny performance area. [21]Being only a few metres from the comedian meant we could see every expression and felt part of the conversation rather than simply watching someone on a stage.[/21] I did become nervous when he started asking the audience questions, but fortunately he chose someone else.||Speaker 4 Man: My aunt took me to an orchestra concert, and I worried I’d be bored because I don't know much about classical music. Several pieces were unfamiliar, although I enjoyed watching all the instruments. [22]Then they began playing music I recognised from a film I had watched repeatedly as a child, and hearing that familiar melody played by a full orchestra was unexpectedly emotional.[/22] I suddenly understood why my aunt loves live music. I've since searched for other concerts with film soundtracks.||Speaker 5 Woman: At a local cultural festival, a dance group performed in the town square. I stopped to watch for a few minutes, assuming that would be all. After their main routine, the dancers invited people forward and demonstrated some easy steps. [23]The highlight for me was joining a huge group of complete beginners and trying the dance myself, rather than standing at the edge simply watching experts perform.[/23] I got several movements wrong, but everyone was smiling and the music made it impossible to feel embarrassed.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: I went to hear a string quartet while on holiday. Classical concerts are not unusual for me because my parents often take me, and the musicians were excellent, as expected. What made this evening different was that they performed inside a huge cave, where small lights illuminated the rock walls and every note echoed around the natural space. It felt completely separate from ordinary life. Even people who didn't usually listen to classical music seemed unable to take their eyes off the performers.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: During a school production, the microphone used by the main actor stopped working in an important scene. At first everyone in the audience looked uncomfortable, because we assumed the show would have to pause. Instead, the other actors immediately moved closer, adjusted their own positions and continued so naturally that the interruption became part of the drama rather than ruining it. I admired their calmness more than any individual line or song. It showed how professional young performers can be.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: I attended a comedy show in a small café rather than a large theatre. I’d seen comedians on television, but live comedy usually seemed expensive and distant. Our seats were directly beside the tiny performance area. Being only a few metres from the comedian meant we could see every expression and felt part of the conversation rather than simply watching someone on a stage. I did become nervous when he started asking the audience questions, but fortunately he chose someone else.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: My aunt took me to an orchestra concert, and I worried I’d be bored because I don't know much about classical music. Several pieces were unfamiliar, although I enjoyed watching all the instruments. Then they began playing music I recognised from a film I had watched repeatedly as a child, and hearing that familiar melody played by a full orchestra was unexpectedly emotional. I suddenly understood why my aunt loves live music. I've since searched for other concerts with film soundtracks.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: At a local cultural festival, a dance group performed in the town square. I stopped to watch for a few minutes, assuming that would be all. After their main routine, the dancers invited people forward and demonstrated some easy steps. The highlight for me was joining a huge group of complete beginners and trying the dance myself, rather than standing at the edge simply watching experts perform. I got several movements wrong, but everyone was smiling and the music made it impossible to feel embarrassed.

options.text: the skill of a young performer
the unusual place where it happened
the way a problem was handled
recognising a familiar piece of music

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The speaker was impressed by experiencing a concert inside a cave.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The speaker admired how smoothly the actors managed a technical failure.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: The intimate experience of sitting very near the comedian made the show memorable.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Hearing a well-known film melody played live was especially meaningful for the speaker.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The speaker most enjoyed being invited to join in with the dancing.
